spec_match.rationale: The concept now listens to both beforeinput and input, showing that the v143 change is input.dataTransfer on contenteditable hosts rather than the older beforeinput path.

interactive.rationale: The page has live contenteditable and textarea targets plus buttons that synthesize insertFromPaste, insertFromDrop, and insertReplacementText events with visible beforeinput/input logs.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature card and concept lede now describe input.dataTransfer and the contenteditable-only scope accurately.

edge_cases.rationale: It covers all three affected inputTypes, compares contenteditable with textarea form controls, reports constructed-event support, and logs null dataTransfer cases explicitly.

references.rationale: The page links to the required chromestatus.com/feature/6715253274181632 entry and the Input Events dataTransfer spec anchor.

design_system.rationale: The CSS uses shared variables, paired button background/color overrides, responsive grids, and a hands-on stage rather than a static code card.

summary: Rebuilt the overview as a two-phase InputEvent inspector: contenteditable and textarea targets, beforeinput vs input logs, all three affected inputTypes, support probes, and synthetic controls that demonstrate the v143 dataTransfer-on-input behavior.
